provenance.source_corpus: Monitoring effects of remediation on natural sediment recovery in Sydney Harbour, Nova Scotia

nodes.name: large-scale dredging of less contaminated outer harbor sediments

source_spans.text: natural recovery of sediments in Sydney Harbour, which were enhanced due to the positive impacts of large-scale dredging of less contaminated outer harbor sediments which were discharged into a confined disposal area located in the inner harbor.

natural recovery of sediments in Sydney Harbour, which were enhanced due to the positive impacts of large-scale dredging of less contaminated outer harbor sediments which were discharged into a confined disposal area located in the inner harbor.

in situ remediation using solidification and stabilization (S/S) by mixing contaminated tar pond sediments with Portland cement, transforming it into a durable, solid, low-hydraulic conductivity matrix to reduce contaminant leaching rates into the surrounding environment

in situ remediation using solidification and stabilization (S/S) by mixing contaminated tar pond sediments with Portland cement, transforming it into a durable, solid, low-hydraulic conductivity matrix to reduce contaminant leaching rates into the surrounding environment
during dredging operations required for construction of new port facilities in the inner Sydney Harbour, sedimentation rates were equivalent to 26 – 128 cm year −1.
during dredging operations required for construction of new port facilities in the inner Sydney Harbour, sedimentation rates were equivalent to 26 – 128 cm year −1.
PAH concentrations increased significantly each year compared to baseline
PAH concentrations increased significantly each year compared to baseline
This resulted in greater abundance and more diverse benthic communities

The annual monitoring of most marine sediment parameters in Sydney Harbour has not detected evidence of substantial contaminant releases into the marine environment as a result of remediation activities at the STP site.
